featureShowcases.heading: Dashboard overview
featureShowcases.summary: The dashboard overview shows StudioDash as a daily review surface for product and provider health.
featureShowcases.body: This screen is intended to reduce context switching for developers who manage multiple apps, websites, and connected services. Instead of starting in a provider console, the user starts with a concise portfolio view and follows the signal that needs attention.
bullets: Combines high-level product status, revenue, website, and sync context.
Makes setup and no-data states visible so missing provider data is clear.
Acts as the entry point for deeper StudioDash analytics pages.

image.alt: StudioDash dashboard overview with portfolio health, revenue, website, and provider status cards
image.caption: A portfolio-first dashboard for scanning the health of connected products and services.

featureShowcases.heading: App and provider analytics
featureShowcases.summary: The app and provider analytics screen shows how StudioDash groups product status with the service signals that explain it.
featureShowcases.body: For a small studio, an app performance change often depends on more than one provider. This view is designed to help connect app status, provider setup, sync state, and available metrics before the user decides which system needs follow-up.
bullets: Keeps provider health close to app-level reporting.
Separates connected, unavailable, placeholder, and planned provider states.
Supports the integrations workflow without overstating live coverage.

image.alt: StudioDash app and provider analytics screen with provider status and app performance panels
image.caption: Provider-aware analytics help explain why a product metric may have changed.

featureShowcases.heading: Revenue and trend review
featureShowcases.summary: The revenue screen focuses on income sources, provider totals, and historical movement.
featureShowcases.body: Developers need to know whether revenue changed because of App Store performance, advertising, website monetisation, a missing provider, or a reporting gap. StudioDash is designed to present those revenue signals together while keeping unavailable data clearly labelled.
bullets: Summarises App Store and advertising revenue where provider data is available.
Supports selected-period review and historical trend comparison.
Keeps setup and no-data messaging visible instead of using placeholder figures.

image.alt: StudioDash revenue analytics screen with provider totals and trend review panels
image.caption: Revenue analytics are built around provider clarity and trend comparison.

featureShowcases.heading: Website and SEO insights
featureShowcases.summary: The website and SEO screen connects site traffic and search visibility with the wider product dashboard.
featureShowcases.body: Product performance is not limited to store dashboards. StudioDash includes website and search signals so developers can review traffic, indexing, Search Console visibility, and Cloudflare analytics alongside revenue and app status.
bullets: Pairs Cloudflare website analytics with Google Search Console visibility.
Helps identify traffic, indexing, and query changes that deserve follow-up.
Fits marketing and website health into the same operating review as app analytics.

image.alt: StudioDash website and SEO analytics screen with traffic and search visibility panels
image.caption: Website and search signals help explain product discovery outside the App Store.

sections.heading: Screenshot set
paragraphs: These screenshots show the current StudioDash dashboard artwork available for the public website.
Future screenshots can replace or expand this set as the product moves closer to public availability.
